OpenTTD-Archipelago-V1.0.0.description:
What's new in OpenTTD Archipelago 1.0.0

Release Overview

OpenTTD Archipelago 1.0.0 is the first full public release following the beta testing period. This version focuses on stability, save/load reliability, and completing the core feature set for the Archipelago multiworld integration.

  • All major beta bugs have been resolved
  • Save/load compatibility has been stabilised
  • The full mission, item, and trap systems are now complete

Major Features

  • 202 vanilla vehicles randomized — all vehicle types across all climates (trains, wagons, road vehicles, aircraft, ships) are locked at game start and distributed into the Archipelago item pool.
  • Iron Horse support — enabling the Iron Horse NewGRF adds 163 additional locomotives to the item pool.
  • 11 procedural mission types — transport cargo, earn profit, build stations, connect towns, maintain station rating, buy from shop, and more.
  • 7 trap items — Breakdown Wave, Recession, Maintenance Surge, Signal Failure, Fuel Shortage, Forced Bank Loan, and Industry Closure.
  • 8 utility items — Cash Injection (£50k/£200k/£500k), Loan Reduction, Cargo Bonus 2×, Reliability Boost, Station Upgrade, and Town Growth Boost.
  • 5 win conditions — Company Value, Monthly Profit, Vehicle Count, Town Population, and Cargo Delivered.
  • DeathLink support — crashes (train, road vehicle, aircraft) send and receive deaths in the Archipelago multiworld.
  • Dynamic pool scaling — mission and shop counts scale automatically based on the number of players (1–24).
  • 56 YAML configuration options — configure map size, climate, economy, vehicle limits, traps, win conditions, and more.

Quality of Life

  • Console location-check commands — use ap_check <Location_Name> and ap_status to inspect Archipelago progression directly from the console.
  • Mission numbers in the mission list — missions now include identifiers like #042, making it easier to match them with spoiler logs.
  • Persistent connection settings — server address and slot name are saved to <personal_dir>/ap_connection.cfg.
  • In-game Guide & Tips window — includes Archipelago commands, hotkeys, and gameplay tips.
  • AP console commands — use commands such as ap !hint <item> directly in the OpenTTD console.

Major Fixes

  • Fixed multiple save/load crashes caused by mismatched field types and chunk parsing errors
  • Fixed Archipelago icon overwriting an internal OpenTTD GUI sprite
  • Fixed vehicle climate filtering issues across all climates
  • Fixed mission generation edge cases and pool scaling errors
  • Fixed several UI and shop display inconsistencies
  • Fixed DeathLink activation issues
  • Improved stability of the Archipelago connection system

Installation

  1. Download openttd-archipelago-v1.0.0-win64.zip
  2. Extract and run openttd.exe
  3. Install openttd.apworld in your Archipelago custom_worlds folder
  4. Connect via the Archipelago button in the main menu

Requirements

  • Windows 10 / 11 (64-bit)
  • Archipelago 0.6.6+

Known Issues

  • No known critical bugs in version 1.0.0
  • Multiplayer (multiple companies) is not supported — run one OpenTTD instance per AP slot
  • Linux/macOS clients currently connect via plain WS (no WSS)
  • The £ symbol in some item names may be platform-dependent on non-UTF-8 Windows locales
OpenTTD-Archipelago-V1.0.0.title: OpenTTD-Archipelago-V1.0.0

v1.0.0-beta1.description:
# OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 1 (v1.0.0-beta1)

First public beta!  
OpenTTD 15.2 with full **Archipelago multiworld randomizer integration**.

---

## Downloads

| File | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `OpenTTD-AP-1.0.0-beta1-windows-x64.zip` | **Game client** — standalone Windows build. Extract and run. Includes OpenGFX + OpenSFX. |
| `openttd.apworld` | **APWorld** — place in your Archipelago `custom_worlds` folder |

---

## Quick Start

1. Download both files above  
2. Place `openttd.apworld` in  
   `C:\ProgramData\Archipelago\custom_worlds\`
3. Extract the ZIP and run `openttd.exe`  
4. Click **Archipelago** in the main menu to connect  

Full instructions: `INSTALL.md`

---

## What's in this release

- 202 randomized vehicles (all climates, all types)
- 11 mission types as checks
- 7 traps + 8 utility items
- 5 win conditions
- Death Link support
- 56 YAML options
- Dynamic scaling for **1–24 players**

---

## Known issues

- **"Maintain X% rating for N months"** is approximated (counts stations, not sustained time)
- WebSocket compression not supported (warning only — connection works)
- Multiplayer (multiple companies) not supported yet

---

## Feedback

This is a **beta build** — feedback is very welcome.

Report bugs or suggestions:

- Archipelago Discord → `#openttd`
- GitHub issues in this repository

---

## Source code

Built from **OpenTTD 15.2** with integrated Archipelago support.

Source code for this build is available in this repository.
v1.0.0-beta1.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 1

v1.2.0.description:
What's new in OpenTTD Archipelago 1.2.0

Release Overview

OpenTTD Archipelago 1.2.0 is a major update that adds multiplayer support, three new game systems, a redesigned status bar, seven additional NewGRF vehicle sets, over 100 new infrastructure unlock items, and a comprehensive rebalance of all win condition targets.

  • New Multiplayer Mode with bridge architecture for cooperative play
  • New Ruins Tracker system — deliver cargo to clear cursed map locations
  • New Demigod System — defeat rival AI companies sent by the God of Wackens
  • New God of Wackens Wrath System — consequences for destructive actions
  • New Colby Event — a multi-step smuggling storyline
  • 7 new NewGRF vehicle sets bundled (Military Items, SHARK Ships, Hover Vehicles, HEQS, Vactrain, Aircraftpack 2025, FIRS Industries)
  • 100+ infrastructure unlock items including track directions, signals, bridges, airports, trees, and terraform
  • Redesigned bottom status bar with integrated AP message log and button bar
  • New Vehicle Index window for browsing all available vehicles
  • Complete win condition rebalance across all 11 difficulty presets
  • Vehicle License Revoke trap added
  • Numerous bug fixes and stability improvements

Compatibility

  • This version is not compatible with multiworld sessions created using version 1.1.0 or earlier.
  • All players must update to the same version before starting a new Archipelago session.
  • The APWorld file has been renamed from openttd.apworld to openttd_exp.apworld.

Major Features

  • Multiplayer Mode (Bridge Architecture)
    Cooperative multiplayer is now supported via a Python bridge application. The bridge connects to the Archipelago server, launches a dedicated OpenTTD server, and synchronizes items, checks, and win condition progress between all connected players. Multiple players share a single company and work toward the same goal. Enable multiplayer_mode in your YAML to use this feature.
  • Ruins Tracker System
    The God of Wackens places cursed ruins on the map. Each ruin requires delivery of 2–4 different cargo types to clear. Clearing a ruin sends a location check. The Ruins Tracker window shows all active ruins with cargo requirements, delivery progress, and nearest town. Click any ruin to scroll the viewport to its location. Configurable: pool size (0–100), max active ruins (1–10), and cargo complexity.
  • Demigod System (God of Wackens)
    The God of Wackens periodically sends rival AI transport companies to challenge the player. Each demigod has a themed company (trains, road, aircraft, ships, or mixed) and must be defeated by paying a tribute. Defeating a demigod sends an Archipelago check. Twenty unique demigod definitions are available. Tribute cost escalates with each successive defeat. Configurable: count (1–10), spawn interval (1–50 years).
  • God of Wackens Wrath System
    When enabled, the God of Wackens watches your destructive actions. Bulldozing houses, removing town roads, and terraforming terrain count against yearly limits. Exceeding limits raises anger through 5 escalating levels — from a gentle warning up to divine wrath with infrastructure destruction, station deletion, and vehicle breakdowns. Each year of good behavior reduces anger by 1 level. Automatically disabled in multiplayer mode.
  • Colby Event
    A multi-step smuggling storyline. A mysterious stranger named Colby asks you to deliver cargo to his town over 5 steps. After the final delivery, you must choose: arrest him for £10M or let him escape. Cargo type and amounts scale with landscape and mission difficulty. Enable with colby_event in your YAML.
  • Infrastructure Unlock Items
    When Sphere Progression is enabled, infrastructure capabilities are locked and must be found as items. This includes 24 rail track direction unlocks, 2 road direction unlocks, 6 signal type unlocks, 13 bridge unlocks, 1 tunnel unlock, 8 airport unlocks, 2 terraform unlocks, 10 tree pack unlocks, and 8 town action unlocks. Individual categories can also be toggled independently via Manual Unlock Toggles.
  • 7 New NewGRF Vehicle Sets
    In addition to Iron Horse, seven more NewGRF sets are now bundled: Military Items (69 aircraft), SHARK Ships (70 ships), Hover Vehicles (6 road vehicles), HEQS (46 road vehicles + 1 train), Vactrain (18 trains), Aircraftpack 2025 (47 aircraft), and FIRS Industries (industry replacement with 5 economy types). All are enabled via YAML toggles and loaded automatically.
  • Redesigned Bottom Status Bar
    The status bar is now a full-width, three-row panel at the bottom of the screen:
    • Top row: News filter buttons (Off/Self/All) and quick-access buttons for Missions, Shop, Ruins, Events, Demigods, Guide, and Vehicle Index
    • Middle row: Last 3 AP messages with a live Checks counter
    • Bottom row: Date, company/pause status, bank balance (original bar)
    AP news messages now appear as text lines in the message log instead of popup windows.
  • Vehicle Index Window
    A searchable catalogue of all vehicles in the current game, filterable by type. Shows each vehicle's name, availability year, and locked/unlocked status.
  • Win Condition Rebalance
    All 11 difficulty presets have been rebalanced with more gradual scaling. Targets now progress smoothly from Casual (£1.5M company value, 40 vehicles) through Madness (£80M company value, 400 vehicles). The 6th win target — Missions Completed — now caps at 80 for Very Hard and above.
  • Vehicle License Revoke Trap
    New trap type: a random vehicle category (trains, road vehicles, aircraft, or ships) is suspended for 1–2 in-game years. All engines of that type are hidden from the build menu until the ban expires.
  • Community Vehicle Names
    When enabled, vehicles you build are automatically named after members of the OpenTTD Archipelago community, with a small chance of a rare funny name.

Gameplay Improvements

  • Shop purchase response time reduced from ~60 seconds to ~1 second (WebSocket recv timeout optimized)
  • Mission generation improved with better cargo and industry validation
  • Mission difficulty now independently scales individual mission target amounts (×0.25 to ×4.0)
  • Mission tier gating: higher difficulty tiers require completing missions from the previous tier
  • Ruin cargo amounts scale with win difficulty (1,000–4,000 at Casual up to 50,000–150,000 at Madness)
  • Improved save/load handling for all new systems
  • AP messages redirected from popup news windows to integrated status bar message log
  • DeathLink deduplication: same death type within 5 seconds is ignored to prevent double-bounce
  • Improved engine locking reliability with periodic safety-net re-lock

New YAML Options

  • multiplayer_mode — enable cooperative multiplayer via bridge
  • enable_sphere_progression — lock all infrastructure behind item finds
  • mission_tier_unlock_count — missions needed to unlock next difficulty tier
  • mission_difficulty — scales individual mission target amounts
  • ruin_pool_size, max_active_ruins, ruin_cargo_types_min/max — ruins configuration
  • enable_demigods, demigod_count, demigod_spawn_interval_min/max — demigod configuration
  • colby_event — enable smuggling storyline
  • enable_wrath, wrath_limit_houses/roads/terrain — God of Wackens wrath system
  • community_vehicle_names — auto-name vehicles after community members
  • 7 NewGRF toggles: enable_military_items, enable_shark_ships, enable_hover_vehicles, enable_heqs, enable_vactrain, enable_aircraftpack, enable_firs
  • firs_economy — FIRS economy type selection
  • Individual infrastructure unlock toggles for when Sphere Progression is disabled
  • World generation: terrain_type, sea_level, rivers, smoothness, variety, number_towns, town_names
  • Economy: economy_type, infrastructure_maintenance, town_cargo_scale, industry_cargo_scale
  • Trap toggle: trap_license_revoke

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed DeathLink protocol causing invalid game state on reconnect
  • Fixed profit sign display showing incorrect values
  • Fixed shop purchase delay caused by 30-second WebSocket recv timeout (now 500ms)
  • Fixed save/reload staging system for mission state
  • Fixed engine locking desync when engines are introduced mid-session
  • Fixed frozen background rendering between status bar buttons (NWID_SPACER replaced with WWT_PANEL)
  • Fixed status bar width not spanning full screen on resize
  • Fixed mission completion sync in multiplayer mode
  • Fixed various save/load serialization issues for new systems
  • Improved overall stability of the Archipelago integration

Known Issues

  • Minor UI clipping may occur at certain window sizes
  • FIRS Industries may produce unexpected cargo combinations in some mission templates
  • Bridge multiplayer requires all players to use the same AP version

Requirements

  • Windows 10 / 11 (64-bit)
  • Archipelago 0.6.6+
  • Python 3.10+ (for bridge multiplayer mode only)
v1.2.0.title: OpenTTD Archipelago v1.2.0

v15.2-1.1.0.description:
What's new in OpenTTD Archipelago 1.1.0

Release Overview

OpenTTD Archipelago 1.1.0 is the first major update following the initial 1.0.0 release. This update expands several core gameplay systems, improves mission generation and progression logic, and resolves multiple issues discovered after the public launch.

  • New dynamic Task System
  • Redesigned Win Condition system
  • New gameplay progression mechanics
  • Improved mission validation and scaling
  • Large number of bug fixes and stability improvements

Compatibility

  • This version is not compatible with multiworld sessions created using version 1.0.0.
  • All players must update to the same version before starting a new Archipelago session.

Major Features

  • Task System
    Dynamic local tasks generated from towns and industries across the map. Tasks appear in the mission window and reward shop currency when completed. Expired tasks are automatically replaced with new ones.
  • Redesigned Win Conditions
    Multiple victory parameters can now be active simultaneously. Players must satisfy all configured conditions to win the game.
  • Difficulty Presets
    11 preset difficulty levels ranging from Casual to Madness. Custom win conditions can also be configured through YAML settings.
  • Speed Boost Items
    Fast-forward speed now starts limited. Speed Boost items gradually increase the maximum game speed.
  • Settings Lockdown
    Gameplay-affecting settings are now locked during active Archipelago sessions to prevent desyncs or exploits.
  • Improved Session Start Flow
    After connecting to the Archipelago server, players are prompted to either generate a new world or load an existing save.

Gameplay Improvements

  • Mission generation improved to reduce invalid industry or cargo targets
  • Improved validation of Archipelago location IDs
  • Better scaling of missions and shop inventory
  • Improved compatibility with Iron Horse vehicle sets
  • Improved save/load handling for missions and tasks

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed Archipelago location ID collisions
  • Fixed incorrect profit calculations in certain missions
  • Fixed mission progress tracking issues
  • Fixed UI refresh issues in mission windows
  • Fixed several save/load serialization issues
  • Fixed mission edge cases discovered during gameplay testing
  • Improved overall stability of the Archipelago integration

Experimental Features

  • Event System
    The event interface and event button are present in the UI and can be triggered during gameplay. However, the underlying event mechanics are not fully functional yet.
  • Players are currently advised not to enable events in the Archipelago settings when generating a world.
  • If events appear during gameplay they can safely be ignored until the system is completed in a future update.

Known Issues

  • Minor UI clipping may occur at certain window sizes
  • Some experimental systems may still receive balancing adjustments

Full Changelog

Mission System

  • Improved mission generation logic
  • Fixed mission generation edge cases
  • Improved validation of mission cargo targets
  • Improved mission progress tracking
  • Improved stability of mission evaluation logic
  • Improved mission pool scaling
  • Improved mission persistence across save/load

Archipelago Integration

  • Improved Archipelago connection stability
  • Improved validation of location IDs
  • Fixed location ID collisions
  • Improved handling of item reception
  • Improved handling of Archipelago state synchronization
  • Improved error handling during connection issues

Gameplay Systems

  • Added dynamic task system
  • Added new win condition framework
  • Added difficulty presets
  • Added speed boost progression items
  • Added session settings lockdown
  • Improved session start workflow

Save / Load

  • Improved serialization for missions
  • Improved serialization for tasks
  • Improved save compatibility handling
  • Fixed several save/load crashes
  • Improved validation during save restoration

User Interface

  • Improved mission window updates
  • Improved UI refresh behavior
  • Fixed multiple minor UI inconsistencies
  • Improved feedback messages in several UI systems

Requirements

  • Windows 10 / 11 (64-bit)
  • Archipelago 0.6.6+
v15.2-1.1.0.title: OpenTTD Archipelago 1.1.0

v15.2-beta2.description:
OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 2 (v15.2-beta2)

WSS support + grafikfix.

Downloads

Fil | Beskrivelse -- | -- openttd-15.2-archipelago-windows-win64.zip | Game client — standalone Windows build. Pak ud og kør. Inkluderer OpenGFX + grafik. openttd.apworld | APWorld — læg den i din Archipelago custom_worlds-mappe

Quick Start

  1. Download begge filer ovenfor
  2. Læg openttd.apworld i
    C:\ProgramData\Archipelago\custom_worlds\
  3. Pak ZIP'en ud og kør openttd.exe
  4. Klik Archipelago i hovedmenuen for at forbinde

Fuld vejledning: INSTALL.md

Nyt i Beta 2

  • WSS-support — klienten forbinder nu krypteret til det officielle archipelago.gg WebHost via wss://
  • wss:// vises som fast label i connect-dialogen så det ikke kan slettes ved en fejl
  • Grafikfix — OpenGFX bundlet direkte i bygningen, ingen "missing sprites"-fejl mere
  • Build-script — ét klik bygger og pakker en komplet release-zip

Hvad er med i denne release

  • 202 randomiserede køretøjer (alle klimaer, alle typer)
  • 11 missionstyper som checks
  • 7 traps + 8 utility items
  • 5 win conditions
  • Death Link support
  • 56 YAML-indstillinger
  • Dynamisk skalering til 1–24 spillere

Kendte problemer

  • "Maintain X% rating for N months" er approksimeret (tæller stationer, ikke vedvarende tid)
  • WebSocket-komprimering ikke understøttet (kun en advarsel — forbindelsen virker)
  • Multiplayer (flere selskaber) ikke understøttet endnu
  • Windows-only build (WSS via Schannel)

Feedback

Dette er en beta-bygning — feedback er meget velkomment.

Rapportér bugs eller forslag:

  • Archipelago Discord → #openttd
  • GitHub Issues i dette repository

Kildekode

Bygget fra OpenTTD 15.2 med integreret Archipelago-support. Kildekode til denne bygning er tilgængelig i dette repository.
v15.2-beta2.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 2

v15.2-beta3.description:
## What's new in Beta 3

### Fixed
- **"Maintain X% rating for N months" missions** now correctly track consecutive months where ALL rated stations meet the threshold. Any station falling below resets the counter to zero. Previously this was approximated by counting qualifying stations.
- **DeathLink notification** now shows a full newspaper popup so it's impossible to miss. Also printed in red in the console.
- **Server field placeholder** no longer shows `wss://` prefix — protocol is auto-detected.

### Added
- **Savegame persistence** — AP session state now survives save/load:
  - Connection credentials (host, port, slot, password)
  - Completed missions
  - Shop page offset and day counter
  - Cumulative cargo and profit statistics
  - "Maintain rating" month counters

### Changed
- Minor dead code and compiler warning cleanup

---

## Installation
1. Download `openttd-15.2-archipelago-windows-win64.zip`
2. Extract and run `openttd.exe`
3. Install `openttd.apworld` in your Archipelago `custom_worlds` folder
4. Connect via the **AP** button in the main menu

## Requirements
- Windows 10/11 x64
- [Archipelago 0.6.6+](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases)

## Known Issues
- WebSocket compression not supported (server logs a warning, no gameplay impact)
- Multiplayer (multiple companies) not supported
- WSS/TLS only on Windows — Linux/macOS falls back to plain WS
v15.2-beta3.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 3

v15.2-beta4.description:
## OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 4

Bug fix release addressing issues reported after Beta 3.

---

### Fixed

- **Wine incompatibility** — Beta 3 broke connections when running under Wine due to the new WSS/WS auto-detection using Windows Schannel APIs that Wine does not implement correctly. Beta 4 detects Wine at runtime and skips the WSS probe entirely, restoring the plain WS behaviour from Beta 2.

- **"Missing 140 sprites" warning on main menu** — A false positive caused by the build not being tagged as a vanilla OpenTTD release. The warning no longer appears. The bundled baseset is complete and gameplay is unaffected.

- **Unviable starting vehicles** — In some seeds, players received a starting vehicle they could not use to earn money:
  - Road vehicle starts could roll a **goods truck**, which requires a factory to supply goods — rarely present near the starting depot.
  - Train starts on non-Toyland maps could roll a **Choo-Choo locomotive**, which does not exist outside Toyland.

  Starting vehicle pools are now restricted to vehicles that are always viable from day one regardless of map industries:
  - **Trains:** steam + early diesel (passengers and mail are always available)
  - **Road vehicles:** buses and mail trucks only
  - **Aircraft:** first 5 small propeller planes (work on a basic airport)
  - **Ships:** passenger ferries only

---

### Known Issues

- Multiplayer (multiple companies) is not supported — run one OpenTTD instance per AP slot.
- WSS (encrypted connections) requires a native Windows build. Wine and Linux builds use plain WS.

---

### Download

Grab the zip below, extract, and run `openttd.exe`. No install required.

The `.apworld` file is inside the `apworld/` folder — copy it to your Archipelago `worlds/` directory before generating.

See the [[setup guide](https://github.com/solida1987/openttd-archipelago/wiki)](https://github.com/solida1987/openttd-archipelago/wiki) for full instructions.
v15.2-beta4.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 4

v15.2-beta5.description:
OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 5

What's new in Beta 5

Fixed

  • Toyland missions on non-Toyland maps — mission generator now filters cargo by climate. Temperate/Arctic/Tropical maps will no longer generate missions involving Candyfloss, Cola or other Toyland-only cargo.
  • Toyland vehicles appearing in item pool on non-Toyland maps — Toyland-exclusive vehicles are now filtered from the item pool unless the map is actually Toyland. Previously these could appear as received items but unlock nothing.
  • Shop purchase bug — shop slots already purchased during the current session could still appear selectable. These are now removed from the shop list immediately after purchase.
  • Shop price display issues — incorrect price display caused by session state desync has been resolved.
  • Service towns mission scaling — “Service X towns” missions are now capped based on map size so impossible numbers cannot be generated.
  • Forced Bank Loan trap scaling — the trap no longer applies a hardcoded £500M loan. It now scales to the configured max_loan value of the session.

Added

  • Starting option: one_of_each — new Starting Vehicle Type option giving the player one safe starting vehicle from each transport type (bus, train, aircraft, ferry) so all transport modes are available from the beginning.
  • Trap Intensity YAML option — new Trap Intensity (%) setting allows control over how many traps appear in the item pool.

Changed

  • DeathLink default is now OFF — previously enabled by default which surprised some players.
  • Default trap configuration toned down
    • Bank Loan trap disabled by default
    • Recession trap disabled by default
  • Shop sorting improved — items are now sorted by price ascending so early-game purchases are easier to find.

Installation

  1. Download openttd-15.2-archipelago-windows-win64.zip
  2. Extract and run openttd.exe
  3. Install openttd.apworld in your Archipelago custom_worlds folder
  4. Connect via the AP button in the main menu

Requirements

  • Windows 10/11 x64
  • Archipelago 0.6.6+

Known Issues

  • WebSocket compression not supported (server logs a warning, no gameplay impact)
  • Multiplayer (multiple companies) not supported — run one OpenTTD instance per AP slot
  • WSS/TLS only supported on Windows — Wine/Linux/macOS use plain WS
v15.2-beta5.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 5

v15.2-beta6.description:
What's new in Beta 6

Added

  • Iron Horse NewGRF support — Iron Horse locomotives can now appear in the Archipelago item pool when the NewGRF is enabled. Engine names are read directly from the GRF data so all locomotives unlock correctly when received.
  • Expanded engine mapping — additional engine lookup logic ensures that engines from NewGRFs and vanilla vehicles can be indexed correctly even if they are expired or not yet introduced in the current year.

Fixed

  • Mission menu text overlapping at UI scale ≥2 — row height is now recalculated every frame instead of cached at window creation, preventing text from overlapping when using larger UI scales.
  • Currency symbol mismatch — mission descriptions previously used a hardcoded £ symbol. The game now displays the correct currency prefix based on the player's selected currency.
  • Random starting vehicle could be unusable — in rare edge cases players could start with a wagon or vehicle that could not be used. Multiple fallback layers now ensure a usable starting vehicle is always available.
  • Shop slots ignoring YAML configuration — the shop now correctly reads the shop_slots value from the YAML configuration instead of using an internal hardcoded value.
  • AP status window stuck in UI — the status window can now be dragged freely and remembers its position between sessions.
  • Vanilla engine lookup failures — engines that returned empty names during engine map construction are now resolved using fallback string IDs.
  • Duplicate wagon name variants — vehicles sharing the same display name (e.g. Rail / Monorail / Maglev variants) now unlock together correctly.
  • Iron Horse engine prefix mismatch — engine lookup now strips the IH: prefix before matching names in the internal engine map.

Changed

  • Trap defaults toned down — the most punishing traps remain disabled by default for new configurations.
  • DeathLink default remains OFF — players must explicitly enable it in YAML.
  • Shop item sorting — items remain sorted by price ascending to make early-game purchases easier to find.

Installation

  1. Download openttd-15.2-archipelago-windows-win64.zip
  2. Extract and run openttd.exe
  3. Install openttd.apworld in your Archipelago custom_worlds folder
  4. Connect via the AP button in the main menu

Requirements

  • Windows 10/11 x64
  • Archipelago 0.6.6+

Known Issues

  • WebSocket compression not supported (server logs a warning, no gameplay impact)
  • Multiplayer (multiple companies) not supported — run one OpenTTD instance per AP slot
  • Linux/macOS/Wine currently connect via plain WS only
v15.2-beta6.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 6

v15.2-beta7.description:
What's new in Beta 7

Added

  • Named destination missions — four new mission types now choose a specific town or industry when the session starts and replace placeholders directly in the mission text:
    • Deliver {amount} passengers to [Town]
    • Deliver {amount} mail to [Town]
    • Supply {amount} tons to [Industry near Town]
    • Transport {amount} tons from [Industry near Town]
    Clicking one of these missions in the missions window will now scroll the map directly to the assigned town or industry. Assignments are also saved and restored correctly across save/load and reconnect.
  • Mission difficulty YAML option — new mission_difficulty option scales monetary and cargo-based mission targets up or down:
    • very_easy ×0.25
    • easy ×0.5
    • normal ×1.0
    • hard ×2.0
    • very_hard ×4.0
  • Starting cash bonus YAML option — new starting_cash_bonus option gives extra starting money on top of the normal loan:
    • none = £0
    • small = £50k
    • medium = £200k
    • large = £500k
    • very_large = £2M
  • Shop price range YAML options — new shop_price_min and shop_price_max settings allow the shop price range to be configured directly instead of using hardcoded values.
  • Sphere progression rules — new accessibility rules in the APWorld help ensure easier progression appears earlier and harder content does not block the early game too aggressively.

Fixed

  • Named missions tracked progress for all companies instead of only the player — progress now uses the cargomonitor APIs so only the player's own deliveries and pickups count toward these missions.
  • Named mission placeholders never resolved — [Town] and [Industry near Town] now correctly resolve because Python and C++ now agree on the mission type strings being used.
  • cargo_to_industry missions could never complete — fixed mission type handling so supply missions now evaluate correctly.
  • cargo_from_industry missions counted all transported cargo instead of the assigned industry — progress now tracks the specific assigned industry only.
  • Named mission names and tiles not restored after save/load — mission names, target tiles, and cargo type are now rebuilt correctly when loading a save or reconnecting.
  • Named missions could remain unresolved after load — missions with IDs but missing names are now assigned properly instead of being skipped.
  • Named missions showed no progress bar at 0 progress — these missions now always show progress text, even when still at 0 / target.
  • Click-to-scroll and mission arrow hint — fixed tile casting issues so the map scroll and visual target hint now work properly.
  • Shop labels showed the player name instead of the item name — shop labels now use item names from slot data.
  • [Town] placeholder issue for specific seeds — world seed handling was corrected to prevent deterministic empty assignments on some map/seed combinations.
  • hint_text property error in Python — fixed the read-only property problem that caused an AttributeError during world generation.
  • DeathLink never activated — the APWorld now correctly sends the death_link value in slot data.
  • Timed effects continued running in menu/pause — timed effects now only count down during active gameplay.
  • Shop rotation did not refresh the UI immediately — the shop now updates properly when rotation changes.
  • C++ location IDs were built from theoretical distribution instead of actual missions — location IDs now match the real generated mission list.
  • Loan Reduction was wasted if the player had already paid off the loan — this now behaves correctly instead of doing nothing.
  • Shop could spam the same utility item repeatedly — repeated duplicate utility appearances are now reduced.
  • location_name_to_id could be too small for large multiworlds — sizing has been corrected for larger sessions.
  • Timed effects reset on save/load — active timed effects now persist correctly.
  • town_population win condition summed total world population — it no longer counts the whole world's population incorrectly.
  • Recession trap could reward players in debt — trap behavior now works properly.
  • Town Growth Boost permanently mutated growth rate — fixed so the effect is temporary as intended.

Changed

  • Shop shows the cheapest items first — item prices are now sorted ascending at world generation time.
  • win_condition_town_population default increased — raised from 20,000 to 50,000.
  • Minimum map size is now 512×512 — 64, 128, and 256 map sizes have been removed because they do not provide enough towns and industries for the new named mission system and often produced impossible mission combinations.

Installation

  1. Download openttd-15.2-archipelago-windows-win64.zip
  2. Extract and run openttd.exe
  3. Install openttd.apworld in your Archipelago custom_worlds folder
  4. Connect via the AP button in the main menu

Requirements

  • Windows 10/11 x64
  • Archipelago 0.6.6+

Known Issues

  • WebSocket compression not supported (server logs a warning, no gameplay impact)
  • Multiplayer (multiple companies) not supported — run one OpenTTD instance per AP slot
  • Windows is still the primary supported build target — non-Windows platforms use plain WS only
v15.2-beta7.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 7

v15.2-beta8.description:
What's new in Beta 8

Added

  • Complete vehicle reference documentation — a full internal reference for all original vehicles, including exact in-game names, string keys, and climate availability, has been added to help prevent future name mismatch issues.

Fixed

  • Iron Horse engines were not locked correctly at session start — Iron Horse vehicle names in slot data use the IH: prefix, while the C++ engine lookup uses the plain in-game name. Both the initial lock pass and the daily re-lock pass now correctly check both forms, so Iron Horse engines stay locked until received.
  • AP server crash caused by shop location count mismatch — C++ previously assumed the number of shop locations was shop_slots × 20, while the APWorld sends the real shop_item_count. This could build invalid location IDs and cause server errors like No location XXXXXXX for player 1. The client now uses the actual shop item count.
  • Starting train vehicles were incorrect for some climates — Temperate and Arctic/Tropic maps could receive starter trains from the wrong climate pool. Starting vehicle selection is now filtered correctly by landscape.
  • Mission and shop windows could only be resized vertically — both windows now support horizontal resizing properly.
  • Several Toyland vehicle pool mistakes were corrected — one non-Toyland vehicle was incorrectly excluded, and multiple Toyland-only vehicles were incorrectly allowed outside Toyland. Vehicle climate filtering has been corrected.
  • cargo_to_industry missions only counted slot 0 cargo — industries that accept multiple cargo types could fail to progress correctly because only the first accepted cargo slot was checked. All valid accepted cargo slots are now counted.
  • cargo_from_industry missions only counted slot 0 cargo — industries producing multiple cargo types could fail to progress correctly because only the first produced cargo slot was checked. All valid produced cargo slots are now counted.
  • Named mission cargo assignment could skip valid industries — named mission assignment and restore logic used slot 0 as a sentinel even when slot 0 was invalid and later slots were valid. Cargo selection now uses the first valid slot instead.
  • Toyland cargo types were missing from cargo lookup — several Toyland cargo names were missing from the internal cargo map, which caused mission evaluation to fall back incorrectly. All missing Toyland cargo labels have now been added.

Changed

  • Beta 8 focuses on core stability — this release primarily targets progression blockers, server-sync issues, mission tracking correctness, and vehicle lock consistency rather than introducing major new gameplay systems.

Installation

  1. Download openttd-15.2-archipelago-windows-win64.zip
  2. Extract and run openttd.exe
  3. Install openttd.apworld in your Archipelago custom_worlds folder
  4. Connect via the AP button in the main menu

Requirements

  • Windows 10/11 x64
  • Archipelago 0.6.6+

Known Issues

  • WebSocket compression not supported (server logs a warning, no gameplay impact)
  • Multiplayer (multiple companies) not supported — run one OpenTTD instance per AP slot
  • Windows is still the primary supported build target — non-Windows platforms use plain WS only
  • New Archipelago branding sprites are planned but not fully working yet
v15.2-beta8.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 8

v15.2-beta9.description:
What's new in Beta 9

Added

  • Console location-check commands — added ap_check <Location_Name> and ap_status to make it easier to inspect Archipelago progression and verify checks directly from the console.
  • Mission numbers are now shown in the mission list — missions now include identifiers like #042 in their descriptions, making it much easier to match them against spoiler logs and search for them quickly.
  • AP connection details are now remembered — the client now saves the last used server address and slot name to <personal_dir>/ap_connection.cfg and restores them automatically the next time the game is opened.

Fixed

  • Critical save/load crash: "Field type is different than expected" — changing station_spread and max_train_length from SLE_UINT8 to SLE_UINT16 caused vanilla and Beta 8 savegames to fail loading because the new binary expected a different stored type. Both fields have been reverted to SLE_UINT8, the AP-side values are clamped correctly, and save/load compatibility is restored.
  • Critical save/load crash: "Broken savegame – Invalid array length" — the AP save chunk loader was missing the final array-termination read, which left the chunk parser in an invalid state and caused load failures. The missing validation step has now been added.
  • Archipelago icon broke the remove-tracks button — an earlier icon replacement accidentally overwrote sprite 714, which is actively used by the rail/road removal GUI. The AP icon now loads into a safe dedicated slot instead.
  • Shop polling could continue forever after hints were resolved — shop hint polling now correctly stops once all hints are loaded.
  • Armored Trucks could spawn incorrectly when custom_vehicle_count=1 — vehicle locking now includes all climate-eligible vehicles instead of only those with direct location slots.
  • Toyland vehicle filtering was still incorrect in some cases — non-Toyland engines could appear in Toyland and Toyland-only vehicles could leak into other climates. Climate filtering has been corrected again.
  • Server address and slot name were not preserved between sessions — connection information is now saved and restored automatically.
  • Named missions could display with the wrong text color — named-destination missions were incorrectly forcing white text and overriding difficulty coloring. They now keep their proper difficulty color.
  • mission_count=300 could produce only 250 missions — pool size calculation previously used a scaling formula instead of respecting the configured value directly. mission_count is now treated as the actual mission count.
  • maintain missions relied on fragile string matching — the APWorld now emits normalized types such as maintain_75 and maintain_90, and the client matches them explicitly.
  • Shop fallback labels could display raw location IDs — when a proper label is unavailable, the shop now shows a clean fallback like Shop Slot #N instead of raw internal identifiers.
  • Sugar, Toys, and Batteries were missing from the Toyland cargo pool — all three cargo types have been added back to the proper Toyland cargo mapping.

Changed

  • Beta 9 focuses on stability and save/load reliability — this release is primarily about eliminating the final savegame blockers, cleaning up UI and mission edge cases, and making the client behave more consistently across sessions.
  • Raised gameplay limits remain in place, but save compatibility is preserved — internal handling was adjusted so custom AP values still work correctly without breaking serialized save data.

Installation

  1. Download openttd-15.2-archipelago-windows-win64.zip
  2. Extract and run openttd.exe
  3. Install openttd.apworld in your Archipelago custom_worlds folder
  4. Connect via the AP button in the main menu

Requirements

  • Windows 10/11 x64
  • Archipelago 0.6.6+

Known Issues

  • No known critical bugs in Beta 9
  • Multiplayer (multiple companies) is not supported — run one OpenTTD instance per AP slot
  • Windows is still the only platform with TLS/WSS support — Linux/macOS currently use plain WS only
  • The £ symbol in some item names may be platform-dependent on non-UTF-8 Windows locales
v15.2-beta9.title: OpenTTD Archipelago — Beta 9
